mission.title: caddy: 2.4.6 regression: presence of symlink breaks file_server browse
mission.description: 2.4.6 regression: presence of symlink breaks file_server browse. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify caddy troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your caddy installation:
```bash
caddy version
caddy validate --config /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review caddy configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant caddy configuration:
It seems that if a symlink exists in a directory, that directory's contents can't be viewed with the browser.  The error is 404.  It happens with valid and broken symlinks.  2.4.5 does not have this issue.

steps.title: Apply the fix for 2.4.6 regression: presence of symlink breaks file_server…
steps.description:
Are you sure your build actually includes the fix?

Try this:

```sh
mkdir /tmp/symlink-test
echo foo > /tmp/symlink-test/foo
ln -s foo /tmp/symlink-test/foo-symlink
caddy file-server -browse -root /tmp/symlink-test -listen :8080
```

Without the fix (version 2.4.6):
```sh
$ curl -I

steps.title: Confirm 2.4.6 regression: presence of symlink breaks… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test caddy to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Are you sure your build actually includes the fix?

Try this:

```sh
mkdir /tmp/symlink-test
echo foo > /tmp/symlink-test/foo
ln -s foo /tmp/symlink-test/foo-symlink
caddy file-server -browse -root /tmp/symlink-test -listen :8080
```

Without the fix (version 2.4.6):
```sh
$ curl -I http://localhost:8080
HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found
Server: Caddy
Date: Fri, 12 Nov 2021 2116 GMT
```
